Tranche Update on ZTO Express Inc.'s Equity Buyback Plan announced on November 14, 2018.
data.description: From July 1, 2025 to September 30, 2025, the company has repurchased 2,020,008 shares, representing 0.25% for $71.7 million. With this, the company has completed the repurchase of 52,919,506 shares, representing 6.5% for $1,300 million under the buyback announced on November 14, 2018.

data.title: Biological E. says WHO grants PQ status to their 14-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine - statement
Jacobs Solutions Inc. Reports Earnings Results for the Fourth Quarter and Full Year Ended September 26, 2025
data.description:
Jacobs Solutions Inc. reported earnings results for the fourth quarter and full year ended September 26, 2025. For the fourth quarter, the company reported sales was USD 3,154.64 million compared to USD 2,960.15 million a year ago. Net income was USD 122.25 million compared to USD 325.44 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 1.06 compared to USD 2.39 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 1.05 compared to USD 2.38 a year ago. Basic earnings per share was USD 0.93 compared to USD 2.52 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share was USD 0.92 compared to USD 2.51 a year ago.
For the full year, sales was USD 12,029.78 million compared to USD 11,500.94 million a year ago. Net income was USD 289.34 million compared to USD 806.09 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 2.59 compared to USD 4.81 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 2.58 compared to USD 4.79 a year ago. Basic earnings per share was USD 2.39 compared to USD 6.35 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share was USD 2.38 compared to USD 6.32 a year ago.

U.S. chipmaker Marvell plans India hiring spree, R&D push to tap AI boom
data.description:
BENGALURU (Reuters) -U.S. chipmaker Marvell Technology plans to boost hiring and research spending in India to tap surging global demand for AI infrastructure, its India head told Reuters.

The company aims to grow its 1,700-strong workforce in India by 15% annually over the next three years, Navin Bishnoi said in an interview on Wednesday, but refrained from giving details of the expanded research and development budget.

Bengaluru serves as Marvell's India headquarters, while its Hyderabad centre focuses on security solutions for data centers. It has a team specialising in embedded development for networking and storage in Pune.

India contributes a small portion of Marvell's revenue, but Bishnoi expects that to grow as local data center capacity expands and data protection laws tighten.

"India is now probably the third largest in data center footprint," Bishnoi said, adding that Marvell is in talks with hyperscalers, or large-scale cloud services providers, and local companies to expand its client base.

As a fabless company that designs advanced chips for AI and cloud infrastructure, Marvell does not manufacture chips but it is in discussions with local assembly and testing firms to align with their manufacturing plans.

These companies, known as outsourced semiconductor assembly and test (OSAT) providers, handle chip packaging and testing after fabrication.

While India is unlikely to become one of Marvell's top revenue markets in the next five years, he sees long-term potential.

"Ten years down the line, yes," Bishnoi said.

Marvell's expansion aligns with Prime Minister Narendra Modi's efforts to make semiconductor manufacturing central to India's economic strategy, reducing import dependence and elevating its role in global electronics.

India does not have a large-scale chip fabrication plant yet, but several projects and OSAT facilities are underway under a $10 billion government incentive programme.

(Reporting by Sai Ishwarbharath B; Editing by Dhanya Skariachan and Saumyadeb Chakrabarty)

By Sai Ishwarbharath B

UK's Starmer to go to China at the end of January, Sky News says
data.description:
LONDON (Reuters) -British Prime Minister Keir Starmer will visit China at the end of January, Sky News reported on Thursday, which would be the first visit to the country by a British leader in seven years.

Starmer's Labour government has made improved relations with Beijing a key priority as it seeks foreign investment to make good on its election pledge to upgrade infrastructure and grow the economy.

But the relationship has been rocked as both countries have traded accusations of perceived spying.

Britain's MI5 security service this week issued a new warning to lawmakers about attempts by Chinese agents to collect information and influence activity.

Former Prime Minister Theresa May was the last British leader to visit China in 2018.

But Britain's relations with China under subsequent Conservative governments were soured by clashes over human rights, Hong Kong and allegations of Chinese espionage.

The prime minister's office declined to comment on a possible Starmer visit.

(Reporting by Sam Tabahriti; Editing by Kate Holton)
